content.Text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 21:36 PDT] Create a new file `docs/fleet-commander.html` for BioBots - a **Printer Fleet Commander** dashboard. This is a standalone HTML file (no external dependencies except docs/shared/nav.js and docs/shared/shared-styles.css which already exist in the repo).

## What to Build

An autonomous multi-printer fleet management dashboard for bioprinting labs with multiple printers. Key features:

### 1. Fleet Overview
- Grid of printer cards showing: name, model, status (idle/printing/maintenance/error/offline), current job, utilization %, health score
- Color-coded status indicators (green=idle, blue=printing, yellow=maintenance, red=error, gray=offline)
- Fleet-wide stats bar: total printers, active jobs, avg utilization, fleet health score

### 2. Utilization Heatmap
- 7-day × 24-hour heatmap grid showing printer utilization (Canvas-rendered)
- Color intensity = utilization level
- Click cell for details

### 3. Autonomous Load Balancer
- Analyzes pending jobs and recommends optimal printer assignment
- Considers: printer capabilities, current load, material compatibility, estimated completion time
- "Auto-assign" button that distributes jobs optimally
- Shows reasoning for each assignment

### 4. Proactive Maintenance Planner
- Tracks print hours, nozzle wear, calibration age for each printer
- Predicts when maintenance is needed using linear projection
- Auto-generates maintenance schedule
- Color-coded urgency (green=OK, yellow=soon, red=overdue)

### 5. Fleet Alerts & Recommendations
- Proactive alert feed with severity levels (info/warning/critical)
- Auto-detected issues: underutilized printers, overworked printers, calibration drift, material waste patterns
- Actionable recommendations with one-click resolution

### 6. Printer Comparison
- Side-by-side comparison of 2-3 printers on throughput, reliability, cost-per-print, uptime

### Simulated Data
Generate realistic simulated data for 6 printers with names like "BioBot Alpha", "BioBot Beta", etc. Each has different specs, utilization patterns, and maintenance states. Include a "Refresh Fleet" button to randomize new data.

## Technical Requirements
- Single self-contained HTML file
- Use Canvas API for the heatmap
- Responsive design, works on desktop
- Include the shared nav: `<script src="shared/nav.js"></script>` and `<link rel="stylesheet" href="shared/shared-styles.css">`
- Modern clean UI with CSS grid/flexbox
- Dark-friendly color scheme consistent with other BioBots tools
- localStorage persistence for fleet configuration
- JSON export of fleet status

## Style
Look at the pattern in other docs/*.html files - they use a consistent style with the shared nav and shared styles. Follow the same pattern.

After creating the file, verify with: `node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const h=fs.readFileSync('docs/fleet-commander.html','utf8'); console.log('Size:',h.length,'Valid HTML:',h.includes('</html>'))"`
